english: claim an edict; mt'ó-ba krims-kyis gnon (mtho ba khrims kyis gnon) he limited the power of the nobility by laws Glr., krims-yig (khrims yig) code of law C.; krims (khrims) also a single precept, rule, commandment Dzl.; Burn. I, 630. — 3. administration of justice, c'ós-kyi krims (chos kyi khrims) the ecclesiastical, dpón-gyi krims (dpon gyi khrims) the secular civil, exercised by the krims-dpón (khrims dpon); lugs gnyis-kyi krims (lugs gnyis kyi khrims) a twofold jurisdiction, a combination of the ecclesiastical and secular administration of justice (as it existed among the Jews); krims srúń-ba (khrims srung ba) to observe, to act according to right, custom, duty; also to exercise jurisdiction, to govern, to reign; to bridle, to keep in check Glr. 95. 9.; krims byéd-pa (khrims byed pa) id. ni f. — tsúl-krims (tshul khrims) a spiritual precept or duty; also a frequent man's name. — 4. action, lawsuit, W. also *t'im-śágs* (*khrims shags*) or only *śágs* (*shags*); gań żig t'im-si p'í-la* (*gang zhig khrims si phyi la*) W. for the sake of some law-suit, *t'im tán-ce* (*khrims gtang ce*) to sit in judgment, to try, to hear causes; *t'im có-pa* (*khrims gcod pa*) C. = *t'im tán-ce* (*khrims gtang ce*) W., means also to pass sentence, to punish, *t'im dág-po tón-wa* (*khrims drag po gtong ba*) to inflict a heavy punishment; mi-la krims-bcad p'og (mi la khrims bcad phog) he incurs, suffers punishment Pth.; *t'im żú-ce* (*khrims zhu ce*) W. to go to law, to commence an action; *t'im żú-kań* (*khrims zhu mkhan*) W. plaintiff; *t'im tán-kań* (*khrims gtang mkhan*) W. magistrate, judge; krims-dpón (khrims dpon) B., C., W., superior judge, chief-justice; *t'im-kyi dág-po* (*khrims kyi bdag po*) C. id.; krims-gyóg (khrims g.yog) apparitor, beadle Cs.; krims-pa (khrims pa) lawyer, advocate Cs. (seems to be little used); krims-kań (khrims khang) court, court of justice, tribunal; krims-ra (khrims ra) id.; place of execution. — 5. use, custom, usage — that power to which people in general show the readiest obedience, and which in every sphere of life forms the greatest obstacle to reforms and improvements.

(krú-ma) (khru ma) (s.) cubit, a measure of eighteen inches, from the elbow to the extremity of the middle finger. The average height of a man is assumed to be four cubits, that of a short man three. — kru jál-ba (khru 'jal ba) to measure with a cubit measure Cs.
a pit filled with corn (?) Sch.; in Mil. kru-slóg-pa (khru slog pa) stands for digging, breaking up the soil, gardening.
1. krul gtoń-ba (khrul gtong ba) to let fall, to drop (several things at intervals), mči-ma (mchi ma) tears Mil. — 2. *da-t'úl* (*zla khrul*) W. intercalary month.
1. W. *k'ú-wa t'úl-ma* (*khu ba khrul ma*) crooked handle, crank (spelling uncertain). — 2. C. a whore.
Sch.: krum-krúm byéd-pa (khrum khrum byed pa), Lt.: krum-krúm brdúń-ba (khrum khrum brdung ba) to pound in a mortar.
and -smád (-smad), two Nakshatras, v. rgyu-skár (rgyu skar).

1. pf. of k'ru-ba ('khru ba). — 2. sbst. bath, washing, ablution; krús-k'u (khrus khu), water for bathing, washing or rinsing; dish-water; krus byéd-pa (khrus byed pa) to bathe, to use baths Dzl.; krús-la gró-ba (khrus la 'gro ba) to go to bathe Dzl.; krus gsól-ba (khrus gsol ba) resp. to take a bath Glr., also to administer a bath to another (cf. gsól-ba (gsol ba)) Glr., Pth.; esp. as a religious ceremony, consisting in the sprinkling with water, and performed, when a new-born infant receives a name, when a person enters into a religious order, or in diseases and on various other occasions (cf. Schl. Buddh. p. 239, where the word is spelled bkrus (bkrus)). Therefore 3. baptism, and krus gsól-ba (khrus gsol ba) to baptize Chr. R. and P. — krús-kyi rdziń (khrus kyi rdzing), pond, pool for bathing; krus-kań (khrus khang) bathing-room or house; krus-sdér (khrus sder) basin, washing-bowl Sch.; krus-búm (khrus bum) sacred watering-pot; krus-gżóń (khrus gzhong) bathing-tub Sch.; krus-gśér (khrus gsher) bath-ing-water Sch., but in Lt. this word re-
